name: Cloudflare XML-RPC Abuse Mitigation Pack
objective: Reduce abuse of WordPress xmlrpc.php endpoints used for pingback amplification and credential brute-force campaigns.
risk_mitigated: DDoS reflection/amplification via XML-RPC pingback abuse
Credential brute-force attempts over XML-RPC methods
Application resource exhaustion from high-frequency XML-RPC POST traffic
assumptions: Application stack includes WordPress or another CMS exposing /xmlrpc.php
Cloudflare WAF custom rules and rate limiting are available on the target plan
Security team can monitor Security Events/Logs during rollout

rules.name: Detect XML-RPC POST access
rules.description: Match POST requests targeting xmlrpc.php to establish baseline and support staged rollout.
rules.notes: Keep in log/simulate mode first to inventory legitimate XML-RPC clients before enforcement.

rules.name: Rate limit XML-RPC pingback abuse
rules.description: Throttle repeated XML-RPC requests likely associated with pingback amplification abuse.
rules.notes: Tune threshold based on observed baseline. Consider lowering to 10/min for sites with no legitimate XML-RPC usage.

rules.name: Rate limit XML-RPC brute-force patterns
rules.description: Apply stricter per-IP threshold to reduce credential stuffing/brute-force attempts over xmlrpc.php.
rules.notes: Start as managed_challenge or log in initial rollout if XML-RPC is required for mobile app publishing workflows.

false_positive_notes: Legacy CMS/mobile publishing clients (e.g., older WordPress mobile apps, Jetpack-like integrations, offline editors) may use xmlrpc.php heavily.
Some trusted plugins perform batch XML-RPC operations that can exceed conservative thresholds.
If XML-RPC is business-critical, allowlist known source IP ranges or service tokens where possible before switching to block.
deployment_notes: Deploy in simulate/log mode first (at least 3-7 days) to build normal traffic baseline.
Review Cloudflare Security Events for xmlrpc.php POST volume, top source IPs/ASNs, and user-agent distribution.
Progressively enforce: log -> managed_challenge -> block, adjusting thresholds to avoid impact on legitimate legacy integrations.
If XML-RPC is unused, prefer explicit block on POST /xmlrpc.php after baseline validation.
monitoring_guidance: Track request count and mitigation events for /xmlrpc.php by source IP and ASN.
Alert on sudden spikes in xmlrpc.php POST rates and repeated mitigations from diverse source ranges.
Correlate with origin CPU/PHP-FPM saturation signals to confirm risk reduction.
